title: salix caprea var. ternata
intro: Salix caprea var. ternata, also known as Kilmarnock willow or Goat willow, is a variety of the goat willow tree (Salix caprea) characterized by its distinctive catkin clusters. The medicinal properties of Salix caprea var. ternata include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its bark and leaves. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Salix caprea var. ternata include a salve or tincture made from its bark, which can be applied topically to soothe skin irritations and promote the healing process.
